content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers.

From array AAA, we create a new array BBB containing all pairwise bitwise ANDs of elements from AAA. That is, BBB consists of N‚ãÖ(N‚àí1)/2N\cdot(N-1)/2N‚ãÖ(N‚àí1)/2 elements, each of the form Ai&AjA_i \mathbin{\&} A_jAi‚Äã&Aj‚Äã for some 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1‚â§i<j‚â§N.

In array BBB, we repeat the following process till there is only one element remaining:


Let the current maximum and minimum element of array BBB be XXX and YYY respectively.
Remove XXX and YYY from array BBB and insert X‚à£YX \mathbin{|} YX‚à£Y into it.
Determine the last remaining element in array BBB.

Here, &\mathbin{\&}& denotes the Bitwise AND operation and ‚à£\mathbin{|}‚à£ denotes the Bitwise OR operation.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of elements in AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the last remaining element in array BBB.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: Array BBB will be [A1&A2]=[1&3]=[1][A_1 \mathbin{\&} A_2] = [1 \mathbin{\&} 3] = [1][A1‚Äã&A2‚Äã]=[1&3]=[1]. There is only one element in BBB so the answer is 111.
Test Case 222: Array BBB will be [A1&A2,A1&A3,A2&A3]=[2&7,2&1,7&1]=[2,0,1][A_1 \mathbin{\&} A_2, A_1 \mathbin{\&} A_3, A_2 \mathbin{\&} A_3] = [2 \mathbin{\&} 7, 2 \mathbin{\&} 1, 7 \mathbin{\&} 1] = [2, 0, 1][A1‚Äã&A2‚Äã,A1‚Äã&A3‚Äã,A2‚Äã&A3‚Äã]=[2&7,2&1,7&1]=[2,0,1].
Then, we do the following operations on BBB:

Remove 222 and 000 from BBB and insert 2‚à£0=22\mathbin{|} 0 = 22‚à£0=2 into it. Now, B=[1,2]B = [1, 2]B=[1,2].
Remove 222 and 111 from BBB and insert 2‚à£1=32\mathbin{|} 1 = 32‚à£1=3 into it. Now, B=[3]B = [3]B=[3].

The last remaining element is thus 333.
